jobDescription: We are notonthehighstreet. Home to over 5000 of the UK’s best small creative businesses, we have been changing the online retail landscape since 2006. Whether you’re working with our Partners to help grow their business, or behind the scenes developing code, we are continuously innovating & working on some really cool stuff all at the same time. And the best bit? We’re making a difference to our Partners’ lives as we do it. We’re about to embark on an ambitious new phase of development to advance our technology, including platform re-architecture to serverless microservices, rewriting the front-end and introducing new big data & machine learning platforms.  It’s an exciting time and we need to strengthen and grow our teams! As an i OS Software Engineer, you will inspire others and champion what can be achieved with mobile. You should be keen to share ideas, expertise and best practice about how we can improve our existing native applications as well as contributing ideas that can help us deliver a first class mobile experience. Some of the things you'll be doing Develop and maintain new features for our existing native i OS apps as well as any new apps that might be required in the future You’ll work collaboratively with other engineers in the team, UX designers, Product Manager and Technical Lead to inform the product’s roadmap, and implement the features and improvements required to bring the i OS app to as many people as possible You’ll make a contribution to defining, maintaining and improving our app, processes and best practices You’ll write Unit and UI automation tests to ensure our app is robust and well tested You’ll use common design patterns and make the best use of the programming language to craft the best possible solution You’ll Pair- Program and work side-by-side with other engineers in the team and business and solve problems collaboratively as well as working on your own Some key skills + experience you’ll need Experience developing i OS apps in a commercial environment You’ll have a basic knowledge of i OS app architecture and be comfortable building apps in Swift You’ll be keen to learn new skills, technologies and languages You’ll have good experience using auto-layout to layout user rich user interfaces You’ll have a basic understanding of common design patterns i.e. MVC and MVVMYou’ll be able to operate in an agile environment, within a small team and be good at managing your own work You’ll have experience using Git and Git Hub to manage source-control You have experience with Swift 4 and keep up-to-date with the latest changes to the language as well as the wider i OS development platform and ecosystem Experience in Objective- C is desirable but not required You’ll have experience with dependency management tools i.e. Cocoapods You’ll have a good understanding of Networking, working with RESTful APIs and consuming JSON. A good understanding of UIKit, Foundation, and other commonly used i OS frameworks Experience with Test- Driven Development, Unit Tests and UI Tests is desirable but not required What we can offer you Because we know how important learning is, we provide the Tech team with online training and time set aside to complete course work, guilds sessions, hackathons and learning groups. On top of this, we offer plenty of fun stuff - staff discount, birthday vouchers, soft/hot drinks on tap, regular Friday drinks and of course our renowned Christmas and Summer parties. As the home of thoughtful gifts, ‘thoughtful’ is our middle name so we also love a surprise or two… (but that’s enough on that for now!) Our perks It’s important to us that our people are well looked after, which is why we offer everyone BUPA healthcare, life insurance and a pension plan. Because having a break is important for your physical and mental wellbeing, our holiday allowance is 25 days (plus bank holidays) AND your entitlement will increase an extra day for every 2 years you spend with us.
